goal: Create the workspace-member `ucil-tests-integration` crate at `tests/integration/` per DEC-0010, then ship `tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs` with four per-fixture integration tests that exercise the LSP diagnostics bridge end-to-end — real fixtures on disk, real `KnowledgeGraph` on tempdir, real `persist_diagnostics` + `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` code paths, local `impl SerenaClient` as the scripted test double at the trait boundary. Flips P1-W5-F08 to passes=true.
plan_summary: Layer 1: add `tests/integration` to the workspace `[members]`, create `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` with a `[[test]] name = "test_lsp_bridge" path = "test_lsp_bridge.rs"` override + minimal dev-deps (tokio, async-trait, lsp-types, serde_json, tempfile, ucil-core, ucil-lsp-diagnostics), create `tests/integration/src/lib.rs` as an empty harness root, remove `tests/integration/.gitkeep`. Layer 2: write `tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs` containing four `#[tokio::test]` async tests — one per fixture project (rust-project, python-project, typescript-project, mixed-project). Each test (a) opens a fresh `ucil_core::KnowledgeGraph` on a `tempfile::TempDir`, (b) constructs a local `impl SerenaClient for LocalScriptedFake` that returns canned `Vec<lsp_types::Diagnostic>` and canned call-hierarchy responses whose URIs point at real files in the fixture's `src/` tree, (c) calls `ucil_lsp_diagnostics::persist_diagnostics(&client, &mut kg, fixture_uri, language)` and asserts one or more rows land in `quality_issues` with the expected file_path (drawn from the fixture path), severity mapping, and category, (d) calls `ucil_lsp_diagnostics::persist_call_hierarchy_incoming(...)` against a seeded symbol and asserts at least one CONTAINS or CALLS row appears in the `relations` table referencing the fixture file path. Layer 3: a supporting `#[test]` that asserts the four per-fixture tests cover all four languages the bridge supports (Python, Rust, TypeScript, and mixed) via a compile-time array — guards against someone quietly dropping a fixture. The `LocalScriptedFake` struct is defined inline (NOT reused from the crate internals — integration tests cannot access `pub(super)` items) and is structurally distinct from mocking Serena's MCP process: it implements UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait exactly as `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` already does inside `quality_pipeline` / `call_hierarchy` (approved pattern per P1-W5-F05 / P1-W5-F06 verifier flips).
scope_in: Cargo.toml (workspace root) — append `"tests/integration"` to the `[workspace].members` array. Keep alphabetical/topological order if present; otherwise append after the `"crates/*"` entries. Do NOT alter other members, profiles, or workspace metadata.

tests/integration/Cargo.toml — NEW FILE. Package manifest per DEC-0010 §'`tests/integration/Cargo.toml` skeleton' (copy verbatim). Key fields: `name = "ucil-tests-integration"`, `version.workspace = true`, `edition.workspace = true`, `authors.workspace = true`, `license.workspace = true`, `repository.workspace = true`, `publish = false`, `description = "UCIL cross-crate integration test binaries (tests/integration/*.rs)."`. `[lib] path = "src/lib.rs"`. `[[test]] name = "test_lsp_bridge" path = "test_lsp_bridge.rs"`. `[dev-dependencies]` block with tokio, async-trait, lsp-types, serde_json, tempfile, ucil-core (path = ../../crates/ucil-core), ucil-lsp-diagnostics (path = ../../crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics). All dev-deps reference `{ workspace = true }` except the two path deps.

tests/integration/src/lib.rs — NEW FILE. Two-line crate-level docstring: `//! UCIL integration-test harness crate — binaries live at \`tests/integration/test_*.rs\`.` + `//! No library API is exposed; this crate exists only to carry \`[[test]]\` entries per DEC-0010.`. Below the doc, attributes copied from sibling crates for consistency: `#![deny(warnings)]` + `#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]`. No other items.

tests/integration/.gitkeep — DELETE. The directory now has real files; `.gitkeep` is redundant and its presence alongside `Cargo.toml` is noise. Use `git rm tests/integration/.gitkeep` in the same commit as the first real file landing.

tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs — NEW FILE. Structure: `#![cfg_attr(not(unix), allow(dead_code))]` to keep the file compilable on Windows even if all tests are `#[cfg(unix)]`-gated (match the server_sharing convention). Crate-level doc cites P1-W5-F08 and DEC-0010. Imports: `async_trait::async_trait`, `lsp_types::{CallHierarchyIncomingCall, CallHierarchyItem, Diagnostic, DiagnosticSeverity, NumberOrString, Position, Range, SymbolKind, Uri}`, `std::path::{Path, PathBuf}`, `std::sync::Mutex`, `ucil_core::KnowledgeGraph`, `ucil_lsp_diagnostics::{diagnostics::{DiagnosticsClientError, SerenaClient}, persist_call_hierarchy_incoming, persist_diagnostics, types::Language}`. Declares a local `struct LocalScriptedFake { diagnostics: Mutex<Vec<(Uri, Vec<Diagnostic>)>>, incoming_calls: Mutex<Vec<(Uri, Vec<CallHierarchyIncomingCall>)>> }` with a constructor `LocalScriptedFake::new()` returning an empty scripted client and a `LocalScriptedFake::push_diagnostics(&self, uri: Uri, diags: Vec<Diagnostic>)` + `LocalScriptedFake::push_incoming(&self, uri: Uri, calls: Vec<CallHierarchyIncomingCall>)` setter. Declares `#[async_trait] impl SerenaClient for LocalScriptedFake` implementing every method defined on the trait — for methods not exercised by a given test, return `Ok(Default::default())` (NOT a stub of the feature: the trait has 4–6 methods, tests exercise 2, the others just satisfy the trait contract; this mirrors the upstream `FakeSerenaClient` / `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` pattern precisely).
tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs — Per-fixture tests (exact names, one per fixture). Each `#[tokio::test]` function:

* `test_rust_project_diagnostics_and_calls` — loads `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/lib.rs` as the target file, builds a `file://` URI via `lsp_types::Uri::from_str(&format!("file://{}", fixture_abs_path.display()))` (canonicalise the fixture path via `std::fs::canonicalize` or `Path::canonicalize` to avoid `./` prefixes on CI). Scripts two diagnostics: one `ERROR` severity with code `E0308` source `rust-analyzer` message `mismatched types` at position (4, 4), one `WARNING` severity with code `42` source `clippy` message `unused variable` at position (10, 10). Opens `KnowledgeGraph::open(tempdir.path().join("kg.sqlite").as_path())`. Calls `persist_diagnostics(&client, &mut kg, fixture_uri, Language::Rust)`; asserts returned count == 2. Queries `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM quality_issues WHERE file_path = ?` with the canonicalised fixture path as parameter; asserts 2. Scripts one incoming-call entry (an `add` symbol callable from `main`), calls `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming(&client, &mut kg, symbol_uri, SymbolKind::FUNCTION, "add")`; asserts `relations` table has ≥ 1 row referencing the fixture file path.

* `test_python_project_diagnostics_and_calls` — same shape, targets `tests/fixtures/python-project/src/python_project/parser.py`, `Language::Python`, canned diagnostics use pyright-style `reportGeneralTypeIssues` source and a `HINT` severity among the two diagnostics.
* `test_typescript_project_diagnostics_and_calls` — same shape, targets `tests/fixtures/typescript-project/src/task-manager.ts`, `Language::TypeScript`, canned diagnostics use `tsserver` source.

* `test_mixed_project_diagnostics_and_calls` — different shape: exercises all three languages in a single test against `tests/fixtures/mixed-project/src/{main.rs,main.py,index.ts}`. Scripts one diagnostic per language URI; calls `persist_diagnostics` three times with the matching `Language` enum; asserts `quality_issues` has exactly 3 rows (one per mixed-project language). Also calls `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` against the `.rs` file's symbol; asserts the relation row's `file_path` ends with `main.rs` (the mixed-project rust half).

tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs — Coverage-guard unit test. `#[test] fn test_suite_covers_four_fixtures()` — asserts a local `const FIXTURES: &[(&str, Language)]` array has exactly 4 entries covering `rust-project / python-project / typescript-project / mixed-project`. Fails the suite if someone silently drops a fixture (defense against future refactor). Same crate, `#[test]` sync (NOT async).

tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs — Rustdoc on `LocalScriptedFake`, on every `#[tokio::test]`, and on the module-level doc explaining: (1) this test file is the P1-W5-F08 acceptance; (2) real fixtures on disk + real KnowledgeGraph on tempdir + real bridge code paths; (3) `LocalScriptedFake` implements UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait (structurally a test DOUBLE of the trait boundary, not a mock of Serena's MCP process); (4) DEC-0010 reference for the file's home.

tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs — Every `.await` on `persist_diagnostics` / `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` must be wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(10), …)` per rust-style.md invariant (every IO await bounded). Use a named const `const BRIDGE_AWAIT_BUDGET: std::time::Duration = std::time::Duration::from_secs(10);`.
scope_out: NO spawning of real `pyright-langserver` / `rust-analyzer` / `typescript-language-server` processes. The `FallbackSpawner` path is exercised elsewhere (`test_fallback_spawn` at `server_sharing::` line 686, already passing under P1-W5-F05) with `sleep`-backed stand-ins; F08 is the PIPELINE contract (bridge → KG persistence), not the spawner contract. A future integration-tester run (or a separate WO for a cross-language LSP smoke test) may add real-LSP spins later.

NO spawning of Docker or Serena container. P1-W5-F08 targets the bridge's local persistence paths; docker-backed Serena is covered by `scripts/verify/serena-live.sh` (Phase 1 gate criterion #4), not by `test_lsp_bridge`.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**` source. Every public item the tests consume (`SerenaClient`, `persist_diagnostics`, `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming`, `Language`, `KnowledgeGraph`, `DiagnosticsClientError`) is already `pub` and re-exported from the crate root. If the test fails because a new item needs public visibility, STOP and escalate — do NOT silently widen visibility.
NO changes to `crates/ucil-core/**` source, `crates/ucil-daemon/**` source, or any other production crate.
NO edits to `tests/fixtures/**` — forbidden per root `CLAUDE.md`.

NO `#[ignore]` / `// SLOW-TEST:` gating. The four tests must run in the default `cargo test --workspace` budget (per-test wall time should be well under 5 s because no real LSP spawning occurs). Ignored tests are disallowed because the selector is `--test test_lsp_bridge` without `--ignored`; an ignored test would silently skip (false green) — forbidden per root `CLAUDE.md` anti-laziness.

NO `todo!()` / `unimplemented!()` / `panic!("stub")` in the `LocalScriptedFake` impl. Every trait method returns a real default value documented in rustdoc (`Ok(Vec::new())` for methods that yield lists, `Ok(None)` for methods that yield optional items, etc.). This mirrors the existing `FakeSerenaClient` pattern in `diagnostics.rs` lines 388-462.
NO edits to `crates/*/tests/**` — the P1-W5-F08 test lives in the new `tests/integration/` crate, not in any existing crate's test dir.
NO renaming of existing files, no file moves outside the new `tests/integration/*` paths.

NO new `[workspace.dependencies]` entries. All required deps (tokio, async-trait, lsp-types, serde_json, tempfile) are already declared workspace-wide — cross-check `Cargo.toml` lines 34, 51, 98, 103.

NO `serde`, `serde_json` round-trip of `lsp_types` payloads in the tests. Construct `lsp_types::Diagnostic` / `CallHierarchyIncomingCall` / `CallHierarchyItem` values directly via their struct literals; matches the existing `make_diag` helper pattern in `quality_pipeline.rs` line 420 (inside `test_fixtures`).

NO reliance on the current working directory at test start. Use `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` from `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` to anchor fixture paths: `Path::new(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")).join("../fixtures/<name>").canonicalize()` — this is deterministic under `cargo test` regardless of invocation dir.

NO `lsp_types::Url` — that import is deprecated in newer `lsp-types`; use `lsp_types::Uri` consistently (the existing bridge code uses `Uri`; confirm via `grep -n "use lsp_types::" crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/*.rs`). If an inconsistency forces `Url`, STOP and escalate.

NO addition of `SerenaClient` trait methods. If `#[async_trait] impl SerenaClient for LocalScriptedFake` fails to compile because a trait method is missing from the impl, add the method by returning a defensible default (see scope_in bullets); do NOT modify the trait itself.
NO `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` wrapper inside the test binary — the file IS the test binary, tests live at module root (DEC-0005 precedent, identical to server_sharing / call_hierarchy).
acceptance: New workspace member `tests/integration` exists; `tests/integration/Cargo.toml`, `tests/integration/src/lib.rs`, and `tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs` are checked in.
`cargo test --test test_lsp_bridge` resolves to exactly one test binary (the new one in `ucil-tests-integration`) and all tests pass.
The binary contains ≥ 4 `#[tokio::test]` async tests (one per fixture) + ≥ 1 synchronous `#[test]` coverage-guard.
`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean — no new warnings introduced in `tests/integration/*` or anywhere else.
`cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` exits 0 with no `error:` or `warning: unresolved link` lines for `ucil-tests-integration`.
Mutation check: `git stash` the body of `persist_diagnostics` in the bridge → any of the four `test_*_project_diagnostics_and_calls` tests fail; `git stash pop` → all pass. (Verifier runs this.)
Every existing test in every other crate continues to pass (workspace-wide nextest green).
acceptance_criteria: cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run --test test_lsp_bridge --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/nextest-WO-0038.log && grep -E 'tests run: ([5-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed' /tmp/nextest-WO-0038.log > /dev/null
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test --test test_lsp_bridge --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/cargotest-WO-0038.log && ! grep -E '^test result: (FAILED|failed)' /tmp/cargotest-WO-0038.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0038.log && ! grep -q '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0038.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo doc --workspace --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0038.log && ! grep -qE '^(error|warning: unresolved)' /tmp/doc-WO-0038.log
grep -c 'async fn test_' tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs | awk '{ if ($1 >= 4) exit 0; else exit 1 }'

master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 5 lines 1763-1770 (LSP diagnostics bridge feature scope, including F08 integration test)

rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (timeouts on every IO await; #[non_exhaustive] error enums; no `.unwrap()` outside #[cfg(test)] — integration tests in #[tokio::test] ARE under #[cfg(test)] semantics)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (~50 LOC per commit soft target; Conventional Commits with Phase/Feature/Work-order trailers)
decision:DEC-0008-lsp-bridge-via-serena-mcp-channel.md (bridge delegates LSP via Serena's MCP channel; 'no duplicate LSP processes' interpreted via SerenaClient trait abstraction)
decision:DEC-0010-tests-integration-workspace-crate.md (THIS WO lands the `tests/integration` workspace crate per DEC-0010; read it before touching Cargo.toml)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (Phase 1 invariants — esp. #1 'no mocks of critical deps', #7 tokio::time::timeout on all IO awaits)

predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0016-diagnostics-quality-pipeline.json (WO-0016 shipped `persist_diagnostics` + the `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` pattern; this WO consumes the same public API from an integration crate)

predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0023-lsp-call-and-type-hierarchy-feed.json (WO-0023 shipped `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` + `call_hierarchy::` tests; this WO drives the same entry points from the integration suite)

file:crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs (lines 322 — `persist_diagnostics`; lines 589-708 — `test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues` as the structural template for the new per-fixture tests)
file:crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs (integration test pattern precedent — `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_…")`-style fixture anchoring, real subprocess usage)

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W5-F05: passes=true (verifier-a9c66996-ed87-4e6f-9113-77026dcbcfaa). `persist_diagnostics` + scripted-client pattern in `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs` provides the structural template.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W5-F06: passes=true (verifier-c14d429c-6fd3-41eb-9c32-0da0bf1163fd). `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming/_outgoing` + `persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes` in `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs` are pub and re-exported from lib root.
dependency_readiness_proof.additional-fixtures-present: `tests/fixtures/typescript-project/src/*.ts` and `tests/fixtures/mixed-project/src/{main.rs,main.py,index.ts}` exist (listed in Phase 0 inventory). Not formally a dep but required by the 4-fixture coverage contract.

lessons_applied: DEC-0005 / WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch avoidance: all `#[tokio::test]` + `#[test]` functions at module root, no `mod tests { }` wrapper in the integration binary. Matches `server_sharing::test_fallback_spawn` precedent (line 686) and `quality_pipeline::test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues` precedent (line 601).

DEC-0008 Serena-delegation interpretation: `SerenaClient` is UCIL's own trait boundary; a local `impl SerenaClient for LocalScriptedFake` is NOT a mock of Serena's MCP process — explicitly aligned with the `FakeSerenaClient` rustdoc at `diagnostics.rs` lines 301-320 ('not a mock of Serena MCP').
WO-0009 / WO-0024 / WO-0025 rustdoc regression: `acceptance_criteria[3]` greps `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` for `^error` or `^warning: unresolved` before verifier spin-up.

WO-0027 PATH-guard / environment-mutation race: this WO does NOT mutate `std::env::*`. Any future per-fixture test that needs PATH isolation MUST coordinate through the crate-wide test_support mutex landed in commit `036e9cf`; if such coordination becomes necessary for a later F08 extension, escalate and do NOT replicate a per-module PATH guard.

WO-0016 `persist_diagnostics` contract: the bridge returns the inserted row count from `persist_diagnostics`; integration tests assert both the returned count AND the `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM quality_issues` row count, matching the precedent in `test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues`.

WO-0023 `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` contract: the bridge writes `relations` rows with typed direction ('CALLS' / 'CONTAINS' etc.). Integration tests query `relations` with `file_path LIKE '%<fixture-filename>'` to avoid brittle absolute-path matches across CI hosts.
